summary: Brazilian novelist writing in Portuguese, best known for the 1988 allegorical novel The Alchemist. Born 1947 in Rio de Janeiro. One of the best-selling authors in history. His works deal with spiritual journeys and self-discovery; his central concept is the 'Personal Legend' — each person's true destiny.

works.name: The Alchemist (1988)
works.description: Coelho's defining work and one of the best-selling novels of all time. An Andalusian shepherd named Santiago dreams of hidden treasure buried beneath the Egyptian pyramids and travels across the Sahara to find it — only to discover the treasure is back where he started. The King of Salem, Melchizedek, gives Santiago mystical black-and-white stones called Urim and Thummim. Santiago works for a crystal merchant in Tangier after being robbed, and joins a caravan across the Sahara where he meets the alchemist. The novel's central message: 'If you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you achieve it.' The prologue retells the myth of Narcissus, where the lake mourns because it can no longer see its own beauty reflected. The title is also shared with a Ben Jonson comedy featuring characters Face and Subtle. Wikipedia

a boy travels across the Sahara on the advice of the King of Salem in order to achieve his Personal Legend

examples: The protagonist tells a gypsy palm reader about his recurring dream before meeting Melchizedek, the King of Salem, who gives him black and white stones for reading omens called Urim and Thummim.
In Coelho's The Alchemist, this character gives Santiago the mystical stones Urim and Thummim.

examples: The business of a crystal merchant in this novel is boosted by cleaning the crystal and putting up a display case.
The protagonist works for a crystal merchant in Tangier after selling his flock in Andalusia and before joining a caravan to cross the Sahara.

clues.clue: Title is shared with a Ben Jonson comedy; Jonson's play features scammers Face and Subtle, who dupe Dapper by claiming he must meet the Queen of Fairy
examples: For 10 points, identify this shared title of a Paulo Coelho novel and a Ben Jonson play.
In Jonson's The Alchemist, Subtle and Face tell Dapper that he will have to consult this mythical character in order to obtain luck in gambling.

clues.clue: Prologue retells the myth of Narcissus — the lake mourns because it can no longer see its own beauty reflected
examples: The prologue of this novel recounts a version of the myth of Narcissus where the lake mourns because it can no longer see its beauty reflected.

clues.clue: After being mugged by thieves, Santiago remembers a sycamore tree outside an abandoned church; the treasure is eventually found under that tree
examples: After being mugged by two thieves, the protagonist of this novel remembers a sycamore tree outside an abandoned church.
A novel with this name ends with Santiago discovering treasure buried under a sycamore tree.

clues.clue: Santiago turns himself into the wind while held by desert militants; earlier he has a vision of oasis attack after seeing one hawk strike another
examples: In this novel, the protagonist sees one hawk attacking another, causing a vision of his oasis coming under siege.
That protagonist was later released from a militant camp after turning himself into the wind.

clues.clue: The novel's central quote: 'If you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you achieve it'

works.name: Veronika Decides to Die (1998)
works.description: 1998 novel about a depressed Slovenian woman named Veronika who attempts suicide and is committed to a mental institution. Mentioned in quizbowl as an identifier for the author. Wikipedia

examples: This author wrote about a depressed Slovenian woman in his 1998 novel Veronika Decides to Die.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Paulo Coelho is a Brazilian author writing in Portuguese, born 1947 in Rio de Janeiro. He is frequently identified in quizbowl as a Brazilian counterpart to Jorge Amado (Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon) and as a writer of internationally bestselling Portuguese-language fiction. His concept of the 'Personal Legend' — each person's individual spiritual calling — is central to his work. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Brazilian author who wrote in Portuguese; contemporary of Jorge Amado
examples: For 10 points, what South American country is home to Paulo Coelho, who wrote The Alchemist in Portuguese?
Coelho wrote in this language, which his Brazilian countryman Jorge Amado used to write Tieta the Goat Girl and Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon.

clues.clue: Central theme: the 'Personal Legend' — the spiritual destiny that the universe helps each person achieve
examples: name this novel where Santiago discovers his Personal Legend, written by Paulo Coelho
a boy travels across the Sahara on the advice of the King of Salem in order to achieve his Personal Legend

comprehensive_summary: Paulo Coelho is a Brazilian novelist born in 1947 in Rio de Janeiro who writes in Portuguese and is one of the best-selling authors of all time. In quizbowl, he is almost exclusively represented by a single landmark work: The Alchemist (1988), an allegorical novel whose shepherd protagonist Santiago dreams of treasure buried under the Egyptian pyramids and embarks on a journey across North Africa to find it, ultimately discovering the treasure was back where he started — near a sycamore tree outside an abandoned church in Andalusia. The Alchemist is dense with quizbowl-ready details: the King of Salem, Melchizedek, gives Santiago two divination stones called Urim and Thummim; Santiago works for a crystal merchant in Tangier after being robbed of his savings; he joins a desert caravan and eventually meets the alchemist who helps him fulfill his 'Personal Legend.' The prologue features an unusual retelling of the Narcissus myth in which the lake grieves not for Narcissus but for its inability to see its own reflection in his eyes. Santiago's climactic transformation into the wind while held by desert militants is another frequently cited detail. The novel shares its title with a Ben Jonson comedy — which has appeared in comparative questions — featuring the con men Face and Subtle and the dupe Dapper, who is told he must consult the Queen of Fairy. Coelho's only other quizbowl-relevant title is Veronika Decides to Die (1998), about a depressed Slovenian woman. In geography and language questions, Coelho is invoked as a representative Brazilian Portuguese-language author alongside Jorge Amado, Clarice Lispector, and Machado de Assis.
